heading: Representation of United States under Convention
label: 5601 Representation of United States under Convention

children.label: A
children.text: be known as a “United States Commissioner to the Northwest Atlantic Fisheries Organization”; and

children.label: B
children.text: serve at the pleasure of the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: at least 1 is appointed from among representatives of the commercial fishing industry;

children.label: ii
children.text: 1 (but no more than 1) is an official of the Government; and

children.label: iii
children.text: 1, other than the individual appointed under clause (ii), is a voting member of the New England Fishery Management Council.

children.label: B
children.text: The Secretary may not appoint as a Commissioner an individual unless the individual is knowledgeable and experienced concerning the fishery resources to which the Convention applies.

children.label: i
children.text: shall be specified by the Secretary at the time of appointment; and

children.label: ii
children.text: may not exceed 4 years.

children.label: B
children.text: An individual who is not a Government official may not serve more than 2 consecutive terms as a Commissioner.

children.heading: Appointment
children.label: 1 Appointment
children.text: The Secretary may, for any anticipated absence of a duly appointed Commissioner, designate an individual to serve as an Alternate Commissioner.

children.heading: Functions
children.label: 2 Functions
children.text: An Alternate Commissioner may exercise all powers and perform all duties of the Commissioner for whom the Alternate Commissioner is designated.

children.heading: Appointment
children.label: 1 Appointment
children.text: The Secretary shall appoint not more than 3 individuals to serve as the representatives of the United States on the Scientific Council, who shall each be known as a “United States Representative to the Northwest Atlantic Fisheries Organization Scientific Council”.

children.label: A
children.text: The Secretary may not appoint an individual as a Representative unless the individual is knowledgeable and experienced concerning the scientific issues dealt with by the Scientific Council.

children.label: B
children.text: The Secretary shall appoint as a Representative at least 1 individual who is an official of the Government.

children.label: A
children.text: shall serve for a term of not to exceed 4 years, as specified by the Secretary at the time of appointment;

children.label: B
children.text: may be reappointed; and

children.label: C
children.text: shall serve at the pleasure of the Secretary.

children.heading: Appointment
children.label: 1 Appointment
children.text: The Secretary may, for any anticipated absence of a duly appointed Representative, designate an individual to serve as an Alternate Representative.

children.heading: Functions
children.label: 2 Functions
children.text: An Alternate Representative may exercise all powers and perform all duties of the Representative for whom the Alternate Representative is designated.

children.heading: Experts and advisers
children.label: e Experts and advisers
children.text: The Commissioners, Alternate Commissioners, Representatives, and Alternate Representatives may be accompanied at meetings of the Organization by experts and advisers.

children.label: A
children.text: coordinate with the appropriate Regional Fishery Management Councils established by ; andsection 1852 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: consult with the committee established under .section 5607 of this title

children.heading: Relationship to other law
children.label: 2 Relationship to other law
children.text: Chapter 10 of title 5 shall not apply to coordination and consultations under this subsection.
